cards.title: Having children makes you smarter
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than their peers. Years of parenting mental and emotional gymnastics appear to provide cognitive benefits despite the chaos.

cards.title: Learning to cope with family holiday anxiety
cards.body: A parent shares how constant worry during family time shifted when a real emergency tested her ability to respond. She discovered she's more capable of handling crises than her anxious mind suggested.

cards.title: Childhood trauma impacts adult relationships
cards.body: Adults raised by narcissistic or avoidant parents often struggle with their own relationships. Learning what healthy connection looks like requires time and often professional support.

cards.title: How to do special time with your child
cards.body: Special Time is 10 minutes of one-on-one interaction where your child leads the activity. This simple practice strengthens connection and presence, even when sitting down to play feels difficult.

cards.title: Are screens delaying basic childhood skills?
cards.body: Teachers report children are struggling to master fundamental life skills compared to previous generations. Increased screen time may be a contributing factor parents should monitor.

cards.title: Understanding attention-seeking adolescent behavior
cards.body: When teens seek attention through behavior, they're actually communicating underlying needs. Adults should recognize these signals as opportunities to address what's really going on.

cards.title: Repairing estrangement after gray divorce
cards.body: When adult children distance themselves from parents following divorce, reconnection is possible with the right approach. Understanding what caused the rift is the first step toward healing.

cards.title: Emotional neglect in childhood still matters today
cards.body: What didn't happen in your childhood—like emotional support or validation—can shape your life as profoundly as traumatic events. Recognizing emotional neglect is the first step to healing.
